WITH min_consumption_CZK AS (
    SELECT CustomerID, MIN(Consumption) AS min_consumption
    FROM yearmonth
    WHERE Date LIKE '2013%' AND Currency = 'CZK'
    GROUP BY CustomerID
),
segment_consumption AS (
    SELECT
        'SME' AS segment,
        (SUM(Consumption) FILTER (WHERE segment = 'SME')) AS total_consumption
    FROM
        yearmonth
        JOIN min_consumption_CZK ON yearmonth.CustomerID = min_consumption_CZK.CustomerID
    WHERE
        segment = 'SME' AND Currency = 'CZK'
),
segment_lam AS (
    SELECT
        'LAM' AS segment,
        (SUM(Consumption) FILTER (WHERE segment = 'LAM')) AS total_consumption
    FROM
        yearmonth
        JOIN min_consumption_CZK ON yearmonth.CustomerID = min_consumption_CZK.CustomerID
    WHERE
        segment = 'LAM' AND Currency = 'CZK'
),
segment_kam AS (
    SELECT
        'KAM' AS segment,
        (SUM(Consumption) FILTER (WHERE segment = 'KAM')) AS total_consumption
    FROM
        yearmonth
        JOIN min_consumption_CZK ON yearmonth.CustomerID = min_consumption_CZK.CustomerID
    WHERE
        segment = 'KAM' AND Currency = 'CZK'
SELECT
    'SME - LAM' AS difference,
    segment_lam.total_consumption - segment_sme.total_consumption AS consumption_difference
FROM segment_sme, segment_lam

UNION ALL

SELECT
    'LAM - KAM',
    segment_kam.total_consumption - segment_lam.total_consumption AS consumption_difference
FROM segment_lam, segment_kam

UNION ALL

SELECT
    'KAM - SME',
    segment_sme.total_consumption - segment_kam.total_consumption AS consumption_difference
FROM segment_sme, segment_kam	----- bird -----	debit_card_specializing

SELECT Segment, AVG(Consumption) as Average_Consumption,
       (SUM(Consumption) OVER (PARTITION BY Segment ORDER BY Date ASC ROWS BETWEEN 1 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) -
        LAG(SUM(Consumption), 1, 0) OVER (PARTITION BY Segment ORDER BY Date ASC) ) as Growth_Percentage
FROM yearmonth
WHERE Date BETWEEN '2012-01-01' AND '2013-12-31'
GROUP BY Segment
ORDER BY Growth_Percentage DESC, Segment ASC
LIMIT 1, 2;


This query calculates the average consumption and the percentage growth in consumption for each segment (SME, LAM, KAM) for the period from 2012 to 2013. The `LIMIT 1, 2` clause is used to return the segment with the highest and the second-highest growth percentage.	----- bird -----	debit_card_specializing

JOIN (
    SELECT Country, Segment FROM gasstations WHERE Country = 'Slovakia'
) as sk_stations
ON gasstations.Country = sk_stations.Country AND gasstations.Segment = sk_stations.Segment

SELECT num_stations_CZ - num_stations_SK as difference, num_discount_CZ - num_discount_SK as discount_difference;	----- bird -----	debit_card_specializing

SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN customers.Currency = 'CZK' THEN customers.CustomerID END) as czk_customers,
       COUNT(DISTINCT customers.CustomerID) as euro_customers
FROM customers
LEFT JOIN transactions_1k ON customers.CustomerID = transactions_1k.CustomerID
WHERE transactions_1k.Price IS NOT NULL AND (customers.Currency = 'CZK' OR transactions_1k.Currency = 'EUR')
GROUP BY customers.Currency
HAVING czk_customers > euro_customers;

SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT customers.CustomerID) - COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN customers.Currency = 'CZK' THEN customers.CustomerID END) as euro_customers,
       COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN customers.Currency = 'CZK' THEN customers.CustomerID END) - COUNT(DISTINCT customers.CustomerID) as czk_customers
FROM customers
LEFT JOIN transactions_1k ON customers.CustomerID = transactions_1k.CustomerID
WHERE transactions_1k.Price IS NOT NULL AND (customers.Currency = 'CZK' OR transactions_1k.Currency = 'EUR')
GROUP BY customers.Currency
HAVING czk_customers > euro_customers;

SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT customers.CustomerID) - COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN customers.Currency = 'CZK' THEN customers.CustomerID END) as euro_customers,
       COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN customers.Currency = 'CZK' THEN customers.CustomerID END) - COUNT(DISTINCT customers.CustomerID) as czk_customers
FROM customers
LEFT JOIN transactions_1k ON customers.CustomerID = transactions_1k.CustomerID
WHERE transactions_1k.Price IS NOT NULL AND (customers.Currency = 'CZK' OR transactions_1k.Currency = 'EUR')
GROUP BY customers.Currency
HAVING czk_customers > euro_customers
LIMIT 1;

SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT customers.CustomerID) - COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN customers.Currency = 'CZK' THEN customers.CustomerID END) - (COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN customers.Currency = 'CZK' THEN customers.CustomerID END) - COUNT(DISTINCT customers.CustomerID)) as difference
FROM customers
LEFT JOIN transactions_1k ON customers.CustomerID = transactions_1k.CustomerID
WHERE transactions_1k.Price IS NOT NULL AND (customers.Currency = 'CZK' OR transactions_1k.Currency = 'EUR')
GROUP BY customers.Currency
HAVING czk_customers > euro_customers
LIMIT 1;


This SQL code will return the difference in the number of customers paying in CZK and EUR, if the number of CZK customers is greater.	----- bird -----	debit_card_specializing

To find the event with the highest ratio of spent to budget, you can use the following SQL query:


SELECT event_id, spent, remaining, (spent / remaining) as ratio
FROM budget
JOIN event ON budget.link_to_event = event.event_id
WHERE status = 'closed'
GROUP BY event_id
ORDER BY ratio DESC
LIMIT 1;

);

    INNER JOIN carcinogenic ON molecule.molecule_id = carcinogenic.molecule_id
)) AS total_chlorine

FROM molecule
INNER JOIN carcinogenic ON molecule.molecule_id = carcinogenic.molecule_id;

FROM (

        INNER JOIN carcinogenic ON molecule.molecule_id = carcinogenic.molecule_id
    )) AS total_chlorine,
